ui.controls: WASD to walk · Shift to run · drag to look · E to interact
ui.diaryHint: Collect a perfume’s full set before delivering it…
ui.eggToast: 🍾 A letter for Venezia, gone to sea.
ui.endingQuestionSub: And your perfect moment — what does it smell like?

morning.story: The male counterpart to Roma — the scent of a morning that finally has shape. Since 1992.

verse.story: A timeless classic of feminine perfumery, in its iconic column-shaped bottle. Since 1988.

flowers.story: Rome, in white flowers — the shy ones, that open only after dark.

knot.story: An infinity knot on the bottle, and one on two hearts by a fountain.

springs.story: The Roman Waters — Ambrosia Aurea, Divinum Ficus, Uva Dulcis, Rubus Maris. A new chapter, written by the next generation.

morning.title: The morning edition
intro: Ah, la Messaggera! You’re early today.
My espresso has gone cold… a morning without coffee has no shape.
Fetch me one from the bar across the square? Mind the pigeons.
outro: Ancora caldo — still warm! You’re an angel.
Now the morning can begin. Grazie, Messaggera.
morning.objective: Fetch an espresso from the bar across the square
prompts: Press E to take the espresso
morning.bloom: Morning light floods the square. The city inhales.

verse.title: Self-love, in verse
verse.giver: La Poetessa — among the laundry lines
intro: The wind! My poem — three pages, blown across the rooftops.
It is a poem to myself. I have never written one of those before.
The last line would not come… perhaps it flew away with the pages. Find them for me?
outro: One… two… three. All of it, back in my hands.
And there — the last line was waiting for me all along:
verse.objective: Find the 3 manuscript pages the wind stole
prompts: Press E to catch the page
Press E to catch the page
Press E to catch the page
verse.bloom: “Loving yourself is where happiness begins.” — her poem, finished at last.

flowers.title: White flowers, after dark
intro: Tonight I host a dinner on my terrace — and my jasmine refuses to open.
White flowers are shy. They wait for the dark to give their perfume.
Light the four lanterns for me. Let’s convince the evening to come early.
outro: Look at them… the jasmine could not resist the lantern light.
Smell that? That is what patience smells like.
flowers.objective: Light the four lanterns on the terrace
prompts: Press E to light the lantern
Press E to light the lantern
Press E to light the lantern
Press E to light the lantern
flowers.bloom: Dusk settles. One by one, the white flowers open.

knot.title: The knot
knot.giver: Il Signore — sulking on the bench
intro: Fifty-one years married, and she pretends the cats are better company.
We argued. About nothing. About everything. You know how it is — no, you’re young, you don’t.
Take her this note. I haven’t the courage to cross the square myself.
outro: …She said that? She really said that?
Ha! Fifty-one years, and she can still make my heart jump. Go on, watch us make up.
knot.objective: Carry the note to her, by the fountain
prompts: Press E to hand her the note
midLines: A note? From that stubborn old man? …Read it to me, my glasses are at home.
“Forgive an old fool who has loved you since before the fountain had water.”
…Hmph. Well. He always did write better than he argued.
knot.choicePrompt: She asks you: what should I reply?
choiceOptions: “Come sit with me, you old fool.”
“You are forgiven — but you’re feeding the cats for a month.”
knot.bloom: The fountain’s jets braid themselves into an endless knot. ∞

springs.title: The three springs of the Aventine
intro: Three marble springs, dry as August. They have not sung in years.
They say each one wakes for a different essence — garden, orchard, vine.
Go to them. Lay your hands on the marble. Perhaps they will sing for a messenger.
outro: Listen… all three, singing together. I never thought I would hear it again.
Green, amber, ruby — the old waters, and a new chapter to write in them.
prompts: Press E to awaken the spring
Press E to awaken the spring
Press E to awaken the spring
springs.bloom: The springs run green, amber and ruby. A new chapter, written by the next generation.

egg.prompt: Press E — there is one more envelope in your bag…
lines: One more envelope. It isn’t on the checklist. The address says only… Venezia.
No bridge reaches Venezia today. Maybe no bridge ever did.
(She slips the letter into an empty bottle, seals it, and lets the water take it.)
“Fuori produzione, non fuori dal cuore. Un giorno, chissà.”

bonus.morning: And you carry bergamot in your bag… you already knew what my morning needed.
bonus.verse: You smell of blackcurrant and lily — you read my poem before I finished it.
bonus.flowers: Neroli and orange blossom, on your hands. The jasmine trusted you at once.
bonus.knot: Rose and pink pepper — the exact scent of our first dance, 1974.
bonus.springs: Chamomile, fig, grape, laurel… you gathered the whole garden. The springs knew your name.
